Description:
Establish prompts/ directory at monorepo root with AGENT.md standards, per-agent prompt files, CONTRACT.md template, and prompt-based relevance filtering.

SCOPE: MVP 1.1
PROJECT AREA: Prompt Engineering
STORIES: 6.1 - 6.5

Create prompts/AGENT.md with XML prompt structure standard and reasoning effort matrix.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Define required XML prompt structure: <role>, <context>, <instructions>, <constraints>, <output_format>, <example>
- Define reasoning effort matrix: scraper=low, ranking=medium, RAG=high, orchestrator=xhigh
- Document prompt versioning convention: semver (MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH)
- Document CONTRACT.md and CHANGELOG.md requirements for every agent
- Add anti-patterns list: no open-ended instructions, no implicit output format, no temperature > 0 for structured outputs
- Minimum 1 positive + 1 negative few-shot example per prompt

EFFORT: M (3 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Prompt Engineering
DEPENDENCIES: JD-1

TESTING CRITERIA:
- XML structure documented with example
- Reasoning effort matrix covers all agent types
- Anti-patterns list actionable and specific

EDGE CASES:
- prompts/ lives at monorepo root — independent of backend deployments

Create prompts/linkedin-agent/ with CONTRACT.md, CHANGELOG.md, system.md, skills.md, tools.md, guardrails.md.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- CONTRACT.md: model=claude-sonnet-4-6, reasoning_effort=low, max_tokens=4000, temperature=0, tools=[playwright_click, playwright_type, playwright_extract], token_budget=~800/invocation
- CHANGELOG.md: v1.0.0 initial release entry
- system.md: XML prompt with <role> LinkedIn scraper, <context> UK senior roles, <instructions>, <constraints>, <output_format> Job schema
- skills.md: Playwright selectors, pagination strategy, anti-bot randomisation
- tools.md: permitted Playwright tools with usage examples
- guardrails.md: no auth, no CAPTCHA bypass, close browser on SIGTERM

EFFORT: M (4 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Prompt Engineering
DEPENDENCIES: JD-32

TESTING CRITERIA:
- All 6 files present
- CONTRACT.md all fields populated
- system.md uses XML structure from prompts/AGENT.md

EDGE CASES:
- Prompt must handle LinkedIn layout changes (fallback selectors documented)

Create prompts/jobserve-agent/ with same six-file structure as LinkedIn agent prompts.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- CONTRACT.md: same structure; jobserve-specific eval set ref
- system.md: JobServe selectors, Next Page pagination, data normalisation rules
- skills.md: JobServe pagination patterns, cookie handling, location normalisation
- tools.md: Playwright tools for JobServe
- guardrails.md: handle empty pages, close browser on failure

EFFORT: M (3 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Prompt Engineering
DEPENDENCIES: JD-32

TESTING CRITERIA:
- All 6 files with JobServe-specific content (not copy-paste from LinkedIn)
- Normalisation rules documented for location field

EDGE CASES:
- Session-based pagination — cookie handling requirement documented

Define the reusable CONTRACT.md template in prompts/AGENT.md documenting all required fields.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Fields: Target Model, Model Version Pinned, Reasoning Effort, Max Output Tokens, Temperature, Permitted Tools, Expected Token Budget, Eval Set Reference, Backward Compatibility policy, Last Regression Run date
- Temperature=0 mandatory for structured outputs — no exceptions without documentation
- Breaking prompt changes increment major version and require new eval set
- Include filled example CONTRACT.md as code block in prompts/AGENT.md

EFFORT: S (1 hour)
PROJECT AREA: Prompt Engineering
DEPENDENCIES: JD-32

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Template present in prompts/AGENT.md
- All fields documented with guidance
- Example uses correct field names

EDGE CASES:
- Eval Set Reference must be a resolvable path

Integrate heuristic prompt-based pre-filtering into scraper agents before pgvector ranking.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Create prompts/linkedin-agent/filtering.md and prompts/jobserve-agent/filtering.md
- Heuristics: seniority (senior/lead/principal/architect), stack (Python/FastAPI/Next.js/TypeScript/Azure/AWS/LLM/AI), contract (contract/freelance/outside IR35)
- Add filter_by_prompt_rules(job: Job) -> bool in filters.py
- Apply before inserting to fake_db in each agent's run()
- Log filtered-out count per agent per run

EFFORT: M (4 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend / Prompt Engineering
DEPENDENCIES: JD-17

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Junior roles filtered out
- Permanent-only roles filtered out
- Filter rules documented in filtering.md
- Filtered count logged

EDGE CASES:
- Warn if filter rate > 90% (overly aggressive)

Set up the evaluation framework for AI agent output quality with DeepEval integration, eval runner script, and CI prompt regression step.

SCOPE: MVP 1.1
PROJECT AREA: AI Quality / Testing
STORIES: 7.1 - 7.3

Create backend/admin/run_evals.py for per-agent eval set execution.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- CLI: --agent {name} argument
- Load eval set from evals/{agent}/eval-set-v1.json
- Each eval case: {input: job_html_snippet, expected_output: Job schema}
- Run agent extraction logic against each input; compare field-level output
- Report: passed/total, failed cases with diff, overall pass rate
- Exit 1 if pass rate below threshold in CONTRACT.md
- Log results as structured JSON

EFFORT: M (4 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend / Admin
DEPENDENCIES: JD-35

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Runs: uv run python admin/run_evals.py --agent linkedin
- Produces pass/fail report with diffs
- Exit 1 on threshold failure

EDGE CASES:
- Missing eval set file: exit 1 with descriptive error
- Empty eval set: warn and exit 0

Wire DeepEval faithfulness and relevance metrics into the eval runner.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Install deepeval as dev dependency
- Use LLMTestCase for each eval case
- Metrics: GEval faithfulness >= 0.85, AnswerRelevancyMetric >= 0.80 (thresholds from CONTRACT.md)
- Integrate into run_evals.py after field-level matching
- Create evals/linkedin/eval-set-v1.json with 5 minimum test cases
- Output combined report: field match rate + DeepEval metric scores

EFFORT: M (5 hours)
PROJECT AREA: AI Quality
DEPENDENCIES: JD-37

TESTING CRITERIA:
- DeepEval metrics run and produce scores
- Threshold enforcement causes exit 1
- eval-set-v1.json valid and loaded

EDGE CASES:
- Document local-mode fallback (no API key required)
- Add --fast flag to skip LLM metrics

Add GitHub Actions eval-regression job to block deploy if quality threshold drops.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Add eval-regression job to ci.yml; trigger on push to main only
- Steps: checkout, uv sync, run_evals.py for linkedin, run_evals.py for jobserve
- Fail on exit code 1
- Upload eval report JSON as artifact
- Timeout: 10 minutes per agent

EFFORT: M (3 hours)
PROJECT AREA: CI/CD
DEPENDENCIES: JD-38, JD-31

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Eval step runs on push to main
- Build blocked if threshold drops
- Artifacts uploaded for audit trail

EDGE CASES:
- LLM API key in CI secrets — document required secret names
- Add retry=1 for flaky evals
